crime.value: Imprisonment and abuse of a woman by Hunsdon, possibly involving drugging and psychological torture
crime.quote: He eased the glass to her mouth and helped her drink... careful of her injuries.

victim.value: An unnamed woman described as beautiful, which Hunsdon considers her 'curse' that makes her a target
victim.quote: Beauty such as yours has been a curse to women since the days of Abraham.

closed_world.value: A room or building where the woman is held captive, unable to escape
closed_world.quote: But rapidly approaching footsteps outside her bedroom brought further speculation... to an abrupt halt.

culprit_and_motive.value: Hunsdon, motivated by a twisted desire to 'save' beautiful women from their 'sinful nature' based on his abusive upbringing
culprit_and_motive.quote: Never fear, my darling, I will never let your soul be lost.

investigator.value: Jewel, who appears to be experiencing supernatural visions or memories while investigating or experiencing the mystery
investigator.quote: You see... nothing. He withdrew his hand and held it out for her inspection.

red_herring.value: Supernatural elements (fire that burns for one character but not another) may be misdirection from human crime
red_herring.quote: He stooped, hesitated, and then put his hand right into the flame before turning to stare at her calmly.

clue_fairness.value: Shared book as physical evidence available to both characters and reader
clue_fairness.quote: You're still holding the book. My book... It is something we can both see—something that is exactly the same

social_world.value: Patriarchal power structure with religious justification for controlling women
social_world.quote: Father said I was a wayward boy... Father never gave up on her. Eventually, he broke her.

reveal_mechanic.value: Physical interaction with supernatural elements as test of reality
reveal_mechanic.quote: He withdrew his hand and held it out for her inspection. Miraculously, it was unscathed.
